page.title: Huntington disease — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Huntington disease: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Huntington disease: 177 alterations and 418 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Efficacy of ethyl-EPA as a treatment for Huntington disease.
Creatine reduces oxidative injury to DNA in patients with Huntington's disease.
Eiocosapentaenoic acid significantly reduces brain atrophy in Huntington's disease.
Huntington disease patients are amenable to a specific exercise-induced therapeutic strategy.
Improved Executive Function and Callosal White Matter Microstructure after Rhythm Exercise in Huntington's Disease.
Pantothenate deficiency could lead to neurodegeneration/dementia in Huntington's disease that might be preventable by treatment with vitamin B5.
Sativex is safe and well tolerated in patients with HD, with no severe adverse events or clinical worsening.
Neurorescue activity, APP regulation and amyloid-beta peptide reduction by novel multi-functional brain permeable iron- chelating- antioxidants, M-30 and green tea polyphenol, EGCG.
6-shogaol against 3-Nitropropionic acid-induced Huntington's disease.
A ketogenic diet delays weight loss and does not impair working memory or motor function in the mouse model of Huntington's disease.
Anti-inflammatory effects of ellagic acid and vanillic acid against quinolinic acid-induced model of Huntington's disease.
Berberine could effectively alleviate motor dysfunction and prolong the survival of transgenic N171-82Q HD mice.
Betulinic acid hydroxamate has the potential for further pharmacological development of a novel drug candidate for the treatment of Huntington's disease.
Cannabigerol could be used for the treatment of neurodegenerative diseases such as Huntington's disease.
Cannabis based medicine could be used as a neuroprotective agent capable of delaying disease progression in Huntington's disease.
Cannabis extracts could be neuroprotective agents, delaying disease progression in a proinflammatory model of Huntington’s disease.
Chrysin ameliorates 3 nitropropinoic acid induced neurotoxicity targeting behavioural, biochemical and histological alterations.
Chrysin can serve as a potential therapeutic agent on 3-Nitropropionic acid induced mitochondrial deficits and subsequent apoptosis.
Coenzyme q10 appears to have significant therapeutic value in a mouse experimental model of Huntington's disease.
Coenzyme Q10 demonstrated significant therapeutic effects in transgenic mouse model of ALS and Huntington's disease.
Combination therapy using CoQ(10) and creatine may be useful in the treatment of neurodegenerative diseases such as Parkinson's disease and Huntington Disease.
Curcumin significantly ameliorates disease symptoms in a Drosophila model of Huntington's disease.
Fullerenols prevent neuron death and reduce oxidative stress in drosophila Huntington's disease model.
Ginsenoside compound K reduces the progression of Huntington's disease.
Ginsenoside Rg1 exerts neuroprotective effects in 3-nitropronpionic acid-induced mouse model of Huntington's disease.
Ginsenosides offer a potential therapeutic choice for the treatment of Huntington's disease.
Goji polysaccharide may be potentially useful for treating Huntington's disease.
Guarana hydroalcoholic extract has therapeutic potential in combating age-related diseases associated with protein misfolding and accumulation.
Huntingtons disease mice infected with T. gondii demonstrate early kynurenine pathway activation, altered immune responses, and premature mortality.
Melatonin inhibits cytosolic mitochondrial-DNA induced neuroinflammatory signaling in accelerated aging and neurodegeneration.
Nano-formulated thymoquinone suspension can efficiently culminate 3-NP induced Huntington's disease progression.
Naringenin mitigates behavioral alterations and provides neuroprotection against 3-nitropropinoic acid-induced Huntington's disease like symptoms.
Neurodegenerative diseases linked directly to mitochondrial malfunction may benefit greatly from CBD-based medicines.
Neuroprotective activity of tetramethylpyrazine against 3-nitropropionic acid induced Huntington's disease-like symptoms.
Neuroprotective effect of aloe emodin against Huntington's disease-like symptoms.
Neuroprotective effects of rutin on ASH neurons in Caenorhabditis elegans model of Huntington's disease.
Neuroprotective potency of mangiferin against 3-nitropropionic acid induced Huntington's disease-like symptoms.
Perillaldehyde alleviates polyQ-induced neurodegeneration through the induction of autophagy and mitochondrial UPR in Caenorhabditis elegans.
Quercetin supplementation is effective in improving mitochondrial dysfunctions induced by 3-nitropropionic acid: implications in Huntington's disease.
Resveratrol may inhibit the progression of motor dysfunction and cognitive impairment in a rat experimental model of Huntington's disease.

disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.
